title: Issue 164 Semantic Patch Run
subtitle: Real-run proof that generated semantic diagnostics can propose, validate, and apply a living-doc patch.
scope: GitHub issue #164 and the typed semantic definitions refactor path in living-doc-compositor.
sourceCoverage: Created from GitHub issue #164 to exercise generated semantic context, relationship gaps, stage diagnostics, valid operations, patch draft validation, patch application, and rendering on a real repo living doc.
objective: Prove that a real living doc update can be driven by generated semantic diagnostics and patch drafts rather than hand-authored JSON edits.
successCondition: The run is successful when MCP infers the surface-delivery semantic graph, reports a relationship gap, returns a valid patch draft, applies it to this JSON document, the original gap disappears, stage diagnostics advance, and the rendered HTML is refreshed.

issueReferences.title: Epic: Typed semantic definitions for living-doc structure and stage diagnostics
Prove generated semantic patch workflow in a real living-doc run

callouts.title: Run result
items: MCP inferred the `surface-delivery` semantic template from this document.
Before application, `living_doc_relationship_gaps` reported `flow-feeds-alignment` as `missing-target-cards` and returned a valid `add-alignment-row` patch draft.

`living_doc_patch_apply` created the alignment card `semantic-patch-workflow-feeds`; after application, the `flow-feeds-alignment` gap was gone and stage diagnostics advanced from Coherence to Judgment.

callout.title: Proof complete
items: The generated graph inferred surface-delivery from the section type sequence.
The missing alignment row was repaired by a generated card-create patch draft.
The original relationship gap disappeared after patch application.

data.name: Generated semantic patch workflow
notes: This card is the source-side entity for the generated `flow-feeds-alignment` relationship.
The alignment card should be created from the MCP relationship-gap patch draft.

data.name: Evidence for Generated semantic patch workflow
notes: Created by the generated `flow-feeds-alignment` patch draft returned from `living_doc_relationship_gaps`.
This card is intentionally minimal: it proves the generated patch path can add the relationship carrier without hand-authoring the repair.

data.name: Contract suite
notes: Run after patch application to prove the path remains covered.
`npm run test:contract` passed after this real-run proof document was updated and rendered.

data.name: Living doc MCP server
data.purpose: Expose semantic context, relationship gaps, stage diagnostics, valid operations, patch validation, patch application, and rendering for this proof run.
